etymology.description: 數(數) (shǔ, "count") + 一(一) (yī, "one/single") + 數(數) (shǔ, "count")
etymology.interpretation: 數(數) (shǔ, "count") expresses the core action of recounting or numbering. When paired with 一(一) (yī, "one/single") and repeated as 數(數) (shǔ), the phrase creates an emphasis on the act of counting, as if to say "count each one attentively." Here, 一(一) (yī, "one/single") highlights the precision in the act of counting, ensuring attention to each single unit.

examples.chinese: 他开始数一数手里的硬币(他開始數一數手裡的硬幣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kāishǐ shǔ yī shǔ shǒulǐ de yìngbì.
examples.english: He started to count the coins in his hand.

examples.chinese: 老师让我们数一数桌上的书(老師讓我們數一數桌上的書)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī ràng wǒmen shǔ yī shǔ zhuō shàng de shū.
examples.english: The teacher asked us to count the books on the table.

examples.chinese: 请你数一数这些苹果(請你數一數這些蘋果)，看有多少个(看有多少個)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng nǐ shǔ yī shǔ zhèxiē píngguǒ, kàn yǒu duōshǎo gè.
examples.english: Please count these apples and see how many there are.
